metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Highly competitive Agreeableness (14th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Low C Conscientiousness (40th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Extraverted Extraversion (66th pct) — Extraverted, outgoing, energetic
Moderate Neuroticism (60th pct)
Conventional Openness to Experience (27th pct) — Conventional, practical, down-to-earth
  Very Low Tender-Mindedness (5th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Low Achievement Striving (32nd pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Low Self-Discipline (36th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  High Activity Level (75th pct) — Fast-paced, energetic, busy
  Moderate Excitement Seeking (54th pct)
  Moderate Positive Emotions (57th pct)
  High Anger / Hostility (72nd pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  High Self-Consciousness (62nd pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Anxious Neuroticism (68th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  High Chronological Age (61st pct)

message.reasoning: We need to simulate a baseline opinion on "Defense_Spending_Increase_Support" for a 61-year-old person with low agreeableness (competitive, skeptical), moderate extraversion, moderate neuroticism (high anger, anxiety), low openness, and very low tender-mindedness. The profile is tough-minded, competitive, and somewhat anxious. Consider how these traits might influence stance on defense spending. Low tender-mindedness suggests less empathy, possibly more hawkish. High anger/hostility and anxiety might support strong defense. Low openness values means conventional, likely to support traditional defense. Extraverted and active, but also competitive. Overall, likely to support increased defense spending. However, the person is also low on achievement striving, so maybe not extremely gung-ho. Also age 61, might lean conservative on security. I'll aim for a moderately strong support, say around 600-700. Avoid round numbers. Let's pick 637. Confidence moderate-high, say 0.78. Reasoning: tough-mindedness, anxiety, and conventional values suggest support for defense increase, but low achievement striving tempers extreme enthusiasm.
